evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: fast-paced products; governance and compliance; testing data models through dbt
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed: velocity framing (fast-paced, new products) balanced with rigour signals (governance, compliance, testing).
evidence.domain_risk: Travel/tourism e-commerce platform; moderate domain risk.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Moderate: e-commerce platform, lacks explicit financial reporting/regulatory language despite governance mention.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineering; Data Science; Analytics Engineers (mentored); other stakeholders across business
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools in responsibilities (dbt, Dagster, Redshift), multiple data disciplines (engineering, science, analytics), growing function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Developing Python and SQL based data pipelines within the Dagster and AWS ecosystem
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: precise technical context naming specific tools (Dagster, AWS, Redshift) and methodologies.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: new products; stakeholder requirements; product analytics
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Product: focus on turning product requirements into data products, supporting new product launches.
evidence.autonomy_level: Mentoring and coaching; working with stakeholders to find new ways to use data
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic: mentorship role, direction-setting around data applications, ownership of team capability building.
evidence.ai_role: Building and maintaining AI Agents using Thoughtspot
evidence.ai_role_explanation: AI enabler: building infrastructure (AI Agents) that downstream systems consume, not using AI as a coding tool.
evidence.testing_framing: documenting and testing data models through dbt
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: testing is framed as owned practice within responsibilities (dbt testing).
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: governance and compliance across the data estate meets regulatory requirements; attention to detail and confidence under pressure
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High loss aversion: governance, compliance, and regulatory language as explicit responsibility; role frames data reliability as critical.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: contributing to DataOps framework and tooling; improving patterns
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mixed: maintaining existing tools (Thoughtspot, Redshift) plus contributing to framework improvements.
evidence.loss_aversion: Making sure governance and compliance across the data estate meets regulatory requirements; high level of attention to detail and confidence to deliver under pressure.
